Healthy.status: The tomato plant is healthy and shows no visible signs of disease.
maintenance_tips: Monitor leaves regularly for unusual spots or discoloration
Maintain good air circulation around plants

causes: Early blight occurs due to a fungal infection caused by Alternaria solani in warm and humid conditions.
The disease spreads rapidly when leaves remain wet for long periods.
Poor air circulation and unhealthy soil conditions increase the risk of infection.
Late blight develops because of the pathogen Phytophthora infestans during cool and wet weather conditions.
Excess moisture and poor ventilation help the disease spread quickly across plants.
Continuous rainfall and damp leaves increase the chances of severe infection.

Healthy.status: The potato crop is healthy and growing under suitable conditions.
maintenance_tips: Inspect plants regularly for disease symptoms
Keep the field free from weeds

causes: Early blight in potato plants is caused by a fungal infection that thrives in warm and humid environments.
Poor soil nutrition and water stress make potato plants more vulnerable to infection.
The disease spreads faster when infected plant debris remains in the field.
Late blight occurs due to infection by Phytophthora infestans in cool and wet conditions.
High humidity and poor drainage encourage rapid disease development.
Continuous wet weather helps the pathogen spread quickly across potato crops.

Healthy.status: The wheat crop is healthy and currently free from visible diseases.
maintenance_tips: Inspect fields during humid weather conditions

causes: Brown rust develops because of a fungal infection caused by Puccinia triticina under warm and humid conditions.
Dense crop growth and excess moisture create a favorable environment for the disease.
The fungus spreads through airborne spores that infect wheat leaves.
Yellow rust is caused by a fungal infection that spreads rapidly during cool and moist weather conditions.
High humidity and dense planting increase the chances of disease spread.
The disease spreads through fungal spores carried by wind and moisture.

Healthy.status: The rice crop is healthy and growing properly without visible disease symptoms.
maintenance_tips: Maintain proper water levels in the field
Use balanced fertilizers for healthy growth
Inspect plants regularly for early disease symptoms
Keep the field clean and free from weeds

causes: Brown spot disease occurs due to fungal infection in rice plants under humid and nutrient-deficient conditions.
Poor soil fertility and lack of proper nutrients increase the chances of infection.
The disease spreads rapidly during continuous wet and warm weather.
prevention: Avoid excessive moisture in the field

causes: Bacterial leaf blight is caused by bacterial infection that spreads quickly in warm and humid environments.
Heavy rainfall and strong winds help the bacteria spread from plant to plant.
Excess nitrogen fertilizer and standing water increase disease severity.
prevention: Monitor crops regularly during rainy seasons
treatment: Reduce standing water in affected areas
